label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Wait, February's last day is usually the 28th, except in a leap year when it's the 29th. But 2001 isn't a leap year, right? Because leap years are divisible by 4. 2000 was a leap year, then 2004. So 2001's February had 28 days. So Jane's birthday is February 28, 2001. Today is her 16th birthday. That means today's date is February 28, 2017, because 2001 + 16 = 2017. But wait, I need to check if 2016 was a leap year. 2016 is divisible by 4, so yes, it was a leap year. But her birthday is on February 28, so even though 2016 had a February 29, her birthday is still the 28th. So on February 28, 2017, she turns 16. The question is asking for the date a month ago from today (her birthday). So if today is February 28, 2017, then a month ago would be January 28, 2017. Let me check that. If you subtract one month from February 28, you go to January 28, because January has 31 days. So the month before February is January, and the 28th exists in both months. So the date would be 01/28/2017. Looking at the options, option D is 01/28/2017. So the answer should be D. Wait, but let me make sure there's no trick here. Sometimes when people say "a month ago," they might subtract 30 days, but in this case, since we're dealing with exact months, it's better to subtract one month.
